body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when an EKS node group role has ECR write permissions. Worker nodes usually need to pull container images, not push, delete, or mutate images in registries.

Restrict node roles to read-only ECR permissions and move image publishing permissions to CI/CD roles.
name: Minimize cluster access to read-only for Amazon ECR
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Remove ECR write permissions from EKS node group roles.

1. Identify the IAM role used by the affected EKS node group.
2. Remove policies that include write actions such as `ecr:PutImage`, `ecr:BatchDeleteImage`, `ecr:InitiateLayerUpload`, `ecr:UploadLayerPart`, and `ecr:CompleteLayerUpload`.
3. Attach the AWS managed policy `AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly` or an equivalent least-privilege read policy.
4. Move image push permissions to a CI/CD role.
5. Re-run sync and assessment.

CLI examples:

```bash
aws iam detach-role-policy --role-name <node-role-name> --policy-arn <ecr-write-policy-arn>
aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name <node-role-name> --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly
```

Cyscale will pass when the EKS node group role no longer has ECR write access.
description: Amazon EKS node group roles that can write to Amazon ECR instead of read-only pulling images.
